(610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2022 Continued To Feb-15-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-04-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gregg D. Zucker, Esq., FOUNDATION LAW GROUP LLP, 310.979.7561, gregg@foundationlaw.com, Attorneys for Plaintiff Jay Stock
Adam J. Karr, Esq., O'MELVENY & MYERS LLP, 213-430-6000, akarr@omm.com, Attorneys for Defendants Dodge & Cox and Dana Emery via ZOOM
Cesar Rodriguez CSR#13269, 909-210-2171, csrodriguez1@msn.com, zoesar21@gmail.com, Court reporter, via ZOOM

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Dodge & Cox's demurrer to complaint is:

Overruled as to causes one and two. Paragraphs 28-29 of the amended complaint clearly allege that plaintiff was terminated because he complained that defendant was attempting to clawback earned wages. The court takes these allegations as true on demurrer. (See Scheimer v. City of Berkeley (2022) 76 Cal.App.5th 549, 554 ["courts treat the demurrer as admitting all material facts properly pleaded"].) The entitlement to wages implicates an established public policy and the pleading meets the requirements of Stevenson v. Superior Ct. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 880, 889-890 [explaining requirements to plead tortious discharge claim]; Gould v. Maryland Sound Indus., Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 1137, 1147 ["[T]he prompt payment of wages due an employee is a fundamental public policy of this state."]; Labor Code sec. 221 [unlawful for employer to retain wages]; Schachter v. Citigroup, Inc. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 610, 618 ["Incentive compensation, such as bonuses and profit-sharing plans, also constitute wages."]

Overruled as to cause of action five. Paragraph 49 alleges that defendant improperly took plaintiff's stock shares. (See Lee v. Hanley (2015) 61 Cal.4th 1225, 1240 [elements of conversion]; Mears v. Crocker First National Bank (1948) 84 Cal.App.2d 637, 644 ["It is also well settled in California that shares of corporate stock are subject to an action in conversion."].)

The prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Cesar Rodriguez CSR#13269, 909-210-2171, csrodriguez1@msn.com, zoesar21@gmail.com, Court reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)
